title: Intensive Family Reunification Services - 211info

Safety and intervention for families in crisis to help children remain in the home or help reunify the family. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Most Children's Programs: Ages 0-5 Child Abuse Prevention and Intervention Services (CAPI): Age 18 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program focuses on early childhood development through case management home visits that include parent education and skill building, developmental and health screenings, kindergarten readiness and transition, and navigating community resources. Safety and intervention for families in crisis to help children remain in the home or help reunify the family. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Most Children's Programs: Ages 0-5 Child Abuse Prevention and Intervention Services (CAPI): Age 18 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (971) 271-6500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO PACIFIC ISLANDER AND ASIAN FAMILY CENTER CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program focuses on early childhood development through case management home visits that include parent education and skill building, developmental and health screenings, kindergarten readiness and transition, and navigating community resources. Safety and intervention for families in crisis to help children remain in the home or help reunify the family. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Most Children's Programs: Ages 0-5 Child Abuse Prevention and Intervention Services (CAPI): Age 18 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER SAFE AND TOGETHER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps families with children in foster care reunite and prevent future foster care placement. Provides intensive home visits and case management and connects families with stabilizing resources. Program works in partnership with birth parents, foster parents, DHS/Child Welfare, and other community partners. 7211 SE 62nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-8 who are in foster care and eligible for reunification Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Closed Friday, December 22, 2023-Monday, January 1, 2024 Email (503) 721-6776 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FOSTER CARE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps Native youth and families involved with foster care by offering cultural support, connecting them to community resources, and assisting with reunification or finding permanent homes. Services include group meetings, support classes, and family events. Also recruits and supports Native American foster families, guiding them through the certification process. Services for foster parents include Positive Indian Parenting classes, Sibling and Family Night events, and help with genealogy and regalia. Does not provide legal advice. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Native American youth ages 0-24 who are in state or tribal foster care systems, their birth parents, and their foster parents. Young adults who have aged out of foster care can receive services through age 24. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROAD TO HOPE MATERNITY HOUSING AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides stable, secure, and nurturing residential care in a home environment for pregnant women and women with children. Residents may stay at any point through their pregnancy and stay up to approximately one year after the birth of their baby. Residents have access to additional support with gaining OB/GYN care, high school/GED education completion, gaining employment, budgeting, individual counseling, advocacy and case management, developing parenting skills, and support working towards family reunification. Participation in faith-based worship service of choosing is required. Accepts mothers with one additional child, when capacity allows. Does not accept couples or individuals with pets of any kind. 2850 SW Cedar Hills Boulevard #55 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Pregnant women age 18 or older who are in crisis (homeless, fleeing domestic violence, etc.). Women can be pregnant and have an additional child under the age of 3 depending on program capacity. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 648-4227 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SELF ENHANCEMENT INC SELF ENHANCEMENT INC COMMUNITY & FAMILY PROGRAMS CHILD WELFARE ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supportive services to stabilize families, keep children out of foster care, and work toward reunification. Services parenting classes, support groups, case management, advocacy with Child Welfare System, and resource navigation. 310 NE Failing Street Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Must be referred by DHS Child Welfare Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 285-0493 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILLAMETTE FAMILY INC WILLAMETTE FAMILY SUNRISE HOUSE & FAMILIES IN RECOVERY FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers two family programs for those in residential/outpatient substance addiction treatment: -Family Reunion Program for DHS-referred families: Program provides services to assist with preventing children from entering the foster care system and/or being reunited with their families after having been in foster care. Services include family advocacy, visitation, home visits, co-residency for children through age 5 whose mothers are in residential treatment, parent education, and mentoring. -Family Services: During residential treatment, clients have access to family advocates who assist families with parenting-related needs. Eligibility: Families in which one or both parents are in substance abuse and/or mental health treatment through Willamette Family. Priority to families where children are at risk of removal from family to be placed into foster care, or who are in foster care and are being reunited with their parents. Hours: No standard hours (541) 762-4300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS IMPACT NW SAFE AND TOGETHER NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FOSTER CARE SUPPORT ROAD TO HOPE MATERNITY HOUSING AND SUPPORT SELF ENHANCEMENT INC CHILD WELFARE ADVOCACY
